name: Film
description: Movies, directors, actors, and iconic scenes

questions.q: In the 1979 film 'Alien,' Sigourney Weaver plays which crew member of the Nostromo?

source.notes: Full name: Ellen Ripley. Weaver reprised the role in 3 sequels.

questions.q: In the 'Toy Story' films, Tom Hanks provides the voice for which character?

source.notes: Sheriff Woody Pride. First Toy Story released 1995.

questions.q: Who played Hermione Granger in all eight Harry Potter films?

source.notes: Cast at age 11. Films released 2001-2011.

questions.q: Which actress starred as Vivian Ward opposite Richard Gere in 'Pretty Woman' (1990)?

source.notes: Earned $463M worldwide. Launched Roberts to A-list status.

questions.q: Which director created the original 'Star Wars' in 1977?

source.notes: Originally titled 'Adventures of Luke Starkiller.' Budget: $11M, grossed $775M.

questions.q: Arnold Schwarzenegger's famous line 'I'll be back' comes from which 1984 film?

source.notes: Directed by James Cameron. Schwarzenegger originally wanted 'I will be back.'

questions.q: Who played the male lead, Johnny Castle, in the 1987 film 'Dirty Dancing'?

source.notes: Famous line: 'Nobody puts Baby in a corner.'

questions.q: Which sibling filmmakers directed the 1999 sci-fi hit 'The Matrix'?

source.notes: Keanu Reeves starred as Neo. Won 4 Academy Awards.

questions.q: Who directed the 1971 dystopian film 'A Clockwork Orange'?

source.notes: Based on Anthony Burgess's 1962 novel. Kubrick withdrew it from UK release.

questions.q: Who directed 'Back to the Future' (1985)?

source.notes: Spielberg produced. Michael J. Fox replaced Eric Stoltz after 5 weeks of filming.

questions.q: Al Pacino plays drug lord Tony Montana in which 1983 crime film?

source.notes: Directed by Brian De Palma. Famous line: 'Say hello to my little friend.'

questions.q: In 'The Silence of the Lambs,' what is the surname of Jodie Foster's FBI trainee character?

source.notes: Clarice Starling. Film won all 5 major Academy Awards (Big Five) in 1992.

questions.q: Tom Cruise and Kelly McGillis starred in which 1986 film about U.S. Navy fighter pilots?

source.notes: Directed by Tony Scott. Sequel 'Top Gun: Maverick' released 2022.

questions.q: Which Alfred Hitchcock thriller features the famous shower scene?

source.notes: 1960 film. Janet Leigh's murder scene uses 70 camera angles in 45 seconds.

questions.q: Lord of the Rings director Peter Jackson is from which country?

source.notes: Born in Wellington. LOTR trilogy filmed entirely in NZ.

questions.q: Keanu Reeves plays which retired hitman in the 'John Wick' film series?

source.notes: Known as 'Baba Yaga' (The Boogeyman). First film released 2014.

questions.q: Sylvester Stallone wrote and starred in which 1976 boxing film that won Best Picture?

source.notes: Stallone wrote the screenplay in 3 days. Budget: $1M, grossed $225M.

questions.q: Which 1959 epic features a legendary chariot race scene?

source.notes: Won 11 Academy Awards, a record matched by Titanic and LOTR: Return of the King.

questions.q: Who composed the soundtrack for Tim Burton's 1989 'Batman'?

source.notes: Danny Elfman scored the film; Prince did the pop songs ('Batdance,' 'Partyman'). Both contributed.

questions.q: In 'Back to the Future,' what is the name of Doc Brown's dog?

source.notes: Einstein was the first time traveler. The dog in 1955 was named Copernicus.

questions.q: Which actress won an Oscar for her role as Lisa Rowe in the 1999 film 'Girl, Interrupted'?

source.notes: Won Best Supporting Actress. Set in a psychiatric hospital in the 1960s.

questions.q: Natalie Portman stars as a tormented ballet dancer in which 2010 psychological thriller?

source.notes: Won Best Actress. Directed by Darren Aronofsky.

questions.q: Kevin Costner directed and starred in which 1990 Western about a Civil War soldier who befriends a Lakota tribe?

source.notes: Won 7 Academy Awards including Best Picture and Best Director.

questions.q: In 'Kill Bill,' Lucy Liu plays an assassin known by which codename?

source.notes: O-Ren Ishii. Uma Thurman's character is 'Black Mamba' (The Bride).

questions.q: Quentin Tarantino directed which 2019 film set in 1960s Hollywood starring Leonardo DiCaprio and Brad Pitt?
choices: Once Upon a Time in Hollywood

source.notes: Won 2 Academy Awards. Set around the Manson Family murders.
